content.text:
Execute the provided git workflow in PUSH mode for the current repository.

Follow these requirements exactly:
1) Run safety checks:
- git status
- git branch --show-current
- git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u} 2>/dev/null
- git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null
2) Inspect unstaged changes for sensitive files and DO NOT stage any matching:
- .env, .env.*, credentials*, secret*, *.pem, *.key
Report any such files explicitly.
3) Silently exclude temp files from staging:
- tmp*, *.tmp, *.swp, *.swo, *~, .DS_Store
4) For push mode:
- run git diff --stat
- run git log --oneline -5
- if there are local changes to commit, stage files individually with git add <file> only; never git add . or git add -A
- draft a concise commit message focused on why, not what
- commit using HEREDOC message format with trailer:
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
- push with git push origin <current-branch>
5) Never force push, never amend, never skip hooks.
6) If working tree is clean and there is nothing to commit, report "Already in sync." and stop.
7) If pull/rebase/merge conflict or another blocker prevents push, stop and report the exact issue instead of taking risky action.

Return:
- branch name and upstream
- ahead/behind counts if available
- whether a commit was created, with short hash
- files changed / insertions / deletions if available
- any skipped files due to safety checks
- final result in this format:
  Git push complete (<branch> → origin/<branch>)
  Commit: <short-hash>  (if applicable)
  Files:  <N> changed, +<insertions> / -<deletions>
  Skipped: <list of excluded files>  (if any)

If nothing was committed, say "Already in sync."

content.name: bash

arguments.command: git status --short --branch && git branch --show-current && git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u} 2>/dev/null && git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null

content.name: bash

arguments.command:
git add .pi/agent/extensions/agent-team.ts && git commit -F - <<'EOF'
Improve agent-team extension reliability and maintainability

This update clarifies team orchestration behavior so the extension is
more predictable during routine usage and easier to reason about when
iterating on agent workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
